content.problemStatement:
We all know that Chef got a box of NNN chocolates (numbered 111 through NNN) as his birthday present. For each valid iii, the iii-th chocolate has a sweetness value WiW_iWi​. Chef is going to play the following randomised game for fun with his brother:


First, an integer kkk between 111 and NNN (inclusive) is chosen uniformly randomly.
Then, kkk times, Chef chooses a chocolate from the box uniformly randomly and independently (each chocolate may be chosen multiple times). For each iii (1≤i≤k1 \le i \le k1≤i≤k), let's denote the sweetness of the iii-th chocolate chosen by Chef by AiA_iAi​.
Chef's brother does the same thing ― kkk times, he also chooses a chocolate from the box uniformly randomly and independently from each other or Chef's choices (so each chocolate may still be chosen multiple times). For each iii (1≤i≤k1 \le i \le k1≤i≤k), let's denote the sweetness of the iii-th chocolate chosen by Chef's brother by BiB_iBi​.
The score of the game is ∑i=1k∑j=1kgcd(Ai,Bj)\sum_{i=1}^k \sum_{j=1}^k \mathrm{gcd}(A_i, B_j)∑i=1k​∑j=1k​gcd(Ai​,Bj​).
Find the expected value of the score. It can be proved that this expected value can be expressed as a fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​, where PPP and QQQ are positive integers and QQQ is coprime with 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353. You should compute P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353, where Q−1Q^{-1}Q−1 denotes the multiplicative inverse of QQQ modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers W1,W2,…,WNW_1, W_2, \ldots, W_NW1​,W2​,…,WN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer P⋅Q−1P \cdot Q^{-1}P⋅Q−1 modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 2:

When k=1k = 1k=1, the score is 12⋅2=21^2 \cdot 2 = 212⋅2=2.
When k=2k = 2k=2, the score is 22⋅2=82^2 \cdot 2 = 822⋅2=8.
When k=3k = 3k=3, the score is 32⋅2=183^2 \cdot 2 = 1832⋅2=18.
When k=4k = 4k=4, the score is 42⋅2=324^2 \cdot 2 = 3242⋅2=32.

The expected score is 2+8+18+324=15\frac{2+8+18+32}{4} = 1542+8+18+32​=15.
